title: Residential Energy/Water Conservation Rebates - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Utility Assistance > Residential Energy/Water Conservation Rebates 2 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CITY OF PORTLAND CITY OF PORTLAND BUREAU OF ENVIRONMENTAL SERVICES ENVIRONMENTAL SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides environmental services that include treating wastewater, storm water drainage services, working watersheds to reduce storm water pollution, restoring native vegetation, and improving water quality in rivers and streams. Manages the Clean River Rewards and storm water utility discount program, where residential and commercial ratepayers may obtain up to a 100% discount of the on-site storm water charge by managing storm water runoff. Offers workshops on storm water management options and discount calculators to help calculate discount. Has a hotline to report pollution or spills. 1120 SW 4th Avenue Portland, OR 972043713 Eligibility: Clean River Rewards: Residential ratepayer participant must own single-family or duplex property; inquire about commercial eligibility Hours: BES Office and Clean River Rewards Hotline: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Automated Clean River Rewards Registration: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 823-7740 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ENERGY TRUST OF OREGON ENERGY CONSERVATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides cash incentives and support for energy-saving and renewable energy projects in homes, industrial, and agricultural businesses. Incentives help pay for upgrades like insulation, duct sealing, heat pumps (which heat and cool homes), and efficient water heaters in single-family and multifamily homes. Some upgrades may also qualify for state and federal tax credits. Also offers free and low-cost tips to help save energy and reduce bills 920 SW Sixth Avenue Suite 900 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Varies by service. Serves Portland General Electric, Pacific Power, Cascade Natural Gas, and Avista customers in Oregon and NW Natural customers in Oregon and Washington. Services are available for homeowners, condo and townhome owners, and renters. Income guidelines vary by program. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (866) 368-7878 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF PORTLAND ENVIRONMENTAL SERVICES ENERGY TRUST OF OREGON ENERGY CONSERVATION SERVICES
